name: Credit Union Of New Jersey, A
description_short: Credit Union of New Jersey is a member-owned financial institution established in 1943, serving over 36,400 members with $442.2M in assets from its Ewing, NJ headquarters.
description_long:
Credit Union of New Jersey was established in 1943 and is headquartered in Ewing, New Jersey. As the 7th largest credit union in New Jersey, it has grown to serve over 36,400 loyal members with assets exceeding $442.2 million. The organization operates with over 81 dedicated employees and maintains a single branch location with 4 ATMs across its network.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), IRAs/KEOGH accounts, credit cards, personal loans, auto loans, business loans, and bill pay services. Members also have access to online loan payments, mobile payments, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and drive-thru teller services. Beyond basic banking, CUNJ provides financial counseling, financial education, financial workshops, and student scholarships.

Credit Union of New Jersey distinguishes itself as a member-owned cooperative focused on serving public sector employees with specialized financial services. The institution offers relatively high account volumes across multiple product lines (47,000+ regular savings accounts, 22,500+ share draft accounts, 1,800+ CDs) and maintains financial education and counseling resources for members. Their long operational history since 1943 suggests institutional stability and community integration.

However, recent member reviews raise significant concerns. A long-term member of 26+ years reported unexpected dormancy fees ($5 dormant account fee, $2 paper statement fee, and additional $5 dormancy charges) on inactive savings accounts, prompting complaints to the Banking Commission. The main office holds a 1.0/5 star rating based on 4 reviews, with complaints about poor in-person and phone service performance. Limited branch and ATM availability (1 branch, 4 ATMs) may restrict member convenience compared to larger financial institutions.
pros: Established 1943 with 82+ years of operational history and stability
7th largest credit union in New Jersey serving 36,400+ members
Comprehensive product suite including personal loans, auto loans, business loans, and investment accounts
Financial counseling, education, workshops, and student scholarships available
Remote deposit capture, mobile payments, and online banking available
Member-owned cooperative structure focused on public sector employees
cons: Only 1 branch and 4 ATMs limits physical access and convenience
Very low member satisfaction rating of 1.0/5 stars based on recent reviews
Dormancy fees and inactivity charges reported by long-term members
Service performance issues noted both in-person and via phone
Currently closed lobby and drive-thru hours at time of reporting
best_for: Public sector employees eligible for CUNJ membership seeking comprehensive banking services
Members prioritizing member-owned cooperative structure over large commercial banks
Customers interested in financial counseling and education services
diagnosis: Credit Union of New Jersey is best for public sector employees seeking a member-owned financial institution with comprehensive banking products. However, the critically low 1.0/5 star member rating and reported dormancy fees warrant caution—prospective members should carefully review current policies and service quality before joining, particularly regarding account maintenance requirements and customer service responsiveness.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
